description: arXiv preprint identifier. Covers both modern (YYMM.NNNNN) and pre-April-2007 (subj/YYMMNNN) forms.
WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical classification code.
UK British National Formulary drug code (chemical/product/presentation).
Chemical Entities of Biological Interest identifier (EBI).
ChEMBL molecule, target, or assay identifier.
SEC Central Index Key.
UK Companies House company number.
Cooperative Patent Classification code (joint EPO/USPTO technology classification).
Current Procedural Terminology code.
Committee on Uniform Securities Identification Procedures (US).
ISO 8601 date.
Digital Object Identifier for scholarly works and datasets.
DrugBank molecule identifier.
Experimental Factor Ontology identifier.
Ensembl gene, transcript, or protein identifier.
NCBI Entrez Gene identifier (integer).
US EPA Facility Registry Service identifier for a regulated facility/site (exposed by FRS and ECHO).
Legacy EudraCT clinical trial identifier.
EU CTIS trial identifier (Regulation 536/2014).
US FDA application number (NDA/ANDA/BLA prefix optional; 6-digit body canonical).
Financial Instrument Global Identifier (Bloomberg/OpenFIGI).
US Federal Information Processing Standard code (state or county).
Open Funder Registry DOI (Crossref's funder identifier scheme, a DOI subspace).
HGNC gene symbol (human).
Gene Ontology term identifier (molecular function, biological process, or cellular component).
Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System code.
HCPCS J-code (clinician-administered drug).
Human Metabolome Database identifier for a metabolite.
Human Phenotype Ontology term identifier (phenotypic abnormalities).
Harmonized System commodity code for internationally traded goods (WCO; 6-digit HS, extended nationally to 8-10 digits).
UK Integrated Care Board code.
International Classification of Diseases, 10th revision.
27-character hashed InChI identifier for a chemical structure.
INPADOC/DOCDB simple patent-family identifier grouping equivalent patents across offices.
InterPro protein family or domain identifier.
International Standard Book Number (ISBN-10 or ISBN-13).
International Securities Identification Number.
International Standard Name Identifier (ISO 27729). Covers both persons and organisations; complement to ORCID for non-researchers and library metadata.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Many APIs return alpha-2; map to ISO_3 when joining across sources that use the alpha-3 form.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code (USD, EUR, GBP). Exposed by FRED, Alpha Vantage, Polygon, World Bank, Nasdaq Data Link.
ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (en, fr, ja, zh). Useful for multilingual sources (OpenAlex, NewsAPI, Europe PMC, Common Crawl, Wikipedia).
International Standard Serial Number (journals, serials).
UK Local Authority District code.
Legal Entity Identifier (ISO 17442). Issued by GLEIF for financial counterparties.
LOINC code for laboratory tests, measurements, and clinical observations.
UK Lower-layer Super Output Area.
Microsoft Academic Graph identifier (legacy; still exposed by OpenAlex via ids.mag).
Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities term (adverse events).
Medical Subject Headings term.
miRBase microRNA precursor or mature identifier.
US CDC week-of-year numbering for surveillance.
Mondo Disease Ontology identifier (harmonised disease concept).
UK Middle-layer Super Output Area.
North American Industry Classification System code (2-6 digit). Used across USAspending, SAM.gov, OSHA, EPA, and Census manufacturing series.
NCBI Taxonomy identifier.
ClinicalTrials.gov trial identifier.
National Drug Code (US).
US National Provider Identifier.
EU regional classification code.
Online Mendelian Inheritance in Man identifier (genes and Mendelian disorders).
OMOP Standardized Vocabularies concept id (OHDSI); the standard target that RxNorm/SNOMED/LOINC/ICD map into.
UK procedure classification code.
Researcher identifier.
Orphanet identifier for rare diseases (ORPHAcode).

OpenStreetMap object identifier. OSM IDs are namespaced by object type; store with the type prefix (e.g. "node/240109189") so the type is unambiguous. Bare numeric IDs are ambiguous between node, way, and relation.
Patent publication number (country prefix + number + kind code); the cross-office patent-document key used by EPO OPS, WIPO, Lens, Google Patents, and USPTO.
Protein Data Bank structure identifier.
PubMed Central identifier (open-access biomedical articles).
PubMed identifier.
ProteomeXchange dataset accession for mass-spectrometry proteomics deposits (exposed by ProteomeXchange, PRIDE, PeptideAtlas).
Research Organization Registry identifier (institutions).
dbSNP reference SNP identifier.
RxNorm Concept Unique Identifier (US drug terminology).
Stock Exchange Daily Official List code (UK).
FDA Structured Product Labeling (SPL) document UUIDv4. Used in DailyMed.
UK Standard Industrial Classification (2007 edition). Companies House records SIC codes per company; ONS economic statistics use the same classification.
SNOMED CT clinical concept identifier (SCTID).
Public equity ticker symbol.
Uber-anatomy ontology identifier (cross-species anatomy).
SAM.gov Unique Entity Identifier for US federal award recipients (replaced DUNS in 2022).
UMLS Metathesaurus Concept Unique Identifier; the cross-vocabulary concept spine linking MeSH, SNOMED CT, ICD, RxNorm, and LOINC.
FDA Unique Ingredient Identifier (Substance Registration System).
UniProt protein accession.
Canonical web URL (last-resort join key).
US state two-letter USPS abbreviation. Used by EIA, CMS, CDC state-level data, Census APIs (alongside FIPS).
WHO Universal Trial Number (ICMJE-recommended cross-registry identifier).
Wikidata entity identifier.
Wikipedia article title (URL-encoded; prefix with language code when not unambiguous, e.g. en:Climate_change).
US Census ZIP Code Tabulation Area.
